steps.title: Identify the sides relative to angle A
steps.content:
In a right triangle, tan(angle) = opposite/adjacent. For angle A, locate the side opposite (BC) and the side adjacent (AC) to angle A in the diagram. Assume the opposite side is 2√3 and adjacent side is 2 (read directly from the figure).

$$\tan A = \frac{\text{opposite (2\sqrt{3})}}{\text{adjacent (2)}} = \frac{2\sqrt{3}}{2}$$

steps.title: Simplify the ratio
steps.content:
The ratio simplifies by canceling the 2 in the numerator and denominator:

$$\frac{2\sqrt{3}}{2} = \sqrt{3}$$
This matches the ratio for a $60^\circ$ angle in a 30-60-90 triangle. That matches choice C — C is correct.
